name: Scots

ancestors.notes: Descends from Northumbrian Old English via Early/Middle Scots.

notes: Scots (Scots leid), a West Germanic language of Lowland Scotland and Ulster. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Scots has no single codified orthography — spelling varies by author, region and period, English-influenced spellings compete with traditional ones, and the same digraph (e.g. <ui>, <ou>, <ch>) takes different values across dialects. A dialect-and-orthography-specific map would be required; none is claimed here. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode scot1243), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode scot1243.
